REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF CITES NO AUTHORITY FOR THE IDEA THAT A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION CAN REQUIRE DEFENDANT TO TAKE AFFIRMATIVE STEPS LIKE A WHOLESALE REMEDIATION OF THE MOLD AND WATER DAMAGE AT ISSUE. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF'S EXHIBITS LACK COMPETENT EVIDENCE ATTRIBUTING THE WATER AND MOLD ISSUES TO DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT. PLAINTIFF ALSO HAS AN ADEQUATE REMEDY OF LAW, AND HAS NOT EXPLAINED WHY THEY HAVE NOT OR CANNOT PAY TO REMEDY THE CONDITIONS OR TEMPORARILY RELOCATE. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Jan-30-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
A. Jeanne Grove, Esq. representing Plaintiff Mohsin Ahmmed via Zoom Video Webinar
Plaintiff Mohsin Ahmmed present in court.

Confidential hearing per order of the court.

Hearing held re Motion for Withdrawal of Plaintiff's Attorney of Record:

The court explains to Plaintiff that the attorney-client relationship is very strict in the law.
The court informs both parties that all moving papers and the opposition were reviewed and considered.
Plaintiff requests to read his oral argument in open court for the record and confirms it is the same as what was submitted in court.
Plaintiff reads oral argument in open court.
Before rendering its findings, the court adresses counsel and states that under normal circumstances, the court does not hear from plaintiff especially if there is a counsel on record.
Counsel did not object to the court having plaintiff make his opposition to the motion as pro per. Counsel submits on the reply previously submitted to the court.

The Court finds that the attorney-client relationship has broken down between plaintiff and counsel. The Court cannot compel counsel to represent someone if they do not want to.

The Court rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF MOHSIN AHMMED MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD

is GRANTED.

Counsel to submit proposed order.

Copy of Plaintiff's oral argument sent by email to Counsel and Ma'am Court reporter with permission of the court and plaintiff.

PLAINTIFF MOHSIN AHMMED Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
is DENIED. Plaintiff cites no authority for the idea that a preliminary injunction can require Defendant to take affirmative steps like a wholesale remediation of the mold and water damage at issue. Further, Plaintiff's exhibits lack competent evidence attributing the water and mold issues to Defendant's conduct. Plaintiff also has an adequate remedy of law, and has not explained why they have not or cannot pay to remedy the conditions or temporarily relocate.
